items.briefing:
Microsoft has released patches fixing 137 security flaws this week, including a critical vulnerability in Netlogon — a core Windows authentication service. All staff should ensure their work devices install any pending updates as soon as possible. Separately, cybersecurity experts are warning that measures announced in the Federal Budget may not be keeping pace with the growing threat of AI-powered cyberattacks, which are becoming more sophisticated and harder to detect.

On a positive note, Infosys has opened a new security operations centre in North Sydney, expanding local cyber support for Australian organisations. As AI adoption grows across Australian businesses, so does the risk of AI-assisted scams and phishing. **Practical tip:** If you receive an unexpected email or message asking you to click a link or share information, verify the request directly with the sender before acting.
